diarized_transcript:
[Agent] Thanks for calling Genshin Impact Player Support, this is Chris. How can I help today?
[Caller] I bought a pack that was advertised at 4.99 US dollars but my card got charged 5.12 euros. I'm in Europe. Why the mismatch?
[Agent] Great question — and honestly a source of a lot of confusion during regional promos. Let me pull your account so I can be specific. UID please?
[Caller] 704582103.
[Agent] Thanks, Sofia. I see the transaction — the Blessing of the Welkin Moon promo displayed on the international banner at the 4.99 USD equivalent, but because your billing region is Europe and your payment method is a Euro-denominated card, the charge processed in Euros through the EU storefront pricing tier. The 5.12 EUR is the current EU list price for that SKU, not a converted USD amount.
[Caller] So it's not a currency conversion fee? It's just the EU price?
[Agent] Exactly. The banner shows the promo tag in USD because that's our global reference currency, but actual charges always match your region's list price. Different regions have different list prices set to local market standards. No FX conversion fee is added by us; your bank may add its own if your card wasn't euro-native, but in this case your card is euro, so the 5.12 EUR is the final price — no extras.
[Caller] OK, I understand the mechanic, but I still feel like the display was misleading.
[Agent] That's completely fair feedback, and I'll flag it to our merchandising team — banners should show local currency to avoid exactly this confusion. For this transaction, I want to make sure you feel good about it. I can't refund the charge since it delivered correctly and matches EU pricing, but I'll add a concierge follow-up: a 160-primogem goodwill gift via in-game mail within 24 hours, plus I'll email you a summary of regional pricing so you can predict charges in the future.
[Caller] That's really fair, thank you.
[Agent] You're welcome. Also, if you ever see a banner with USD and want to confirm your local price before buying, the in-game Shop always shows the final amount in your regional currency before checkout — that's the source of truth.
[Caller] Good to know. Thanks Chris.
[Agent] Anytime, Sofia. Have a great day!
